commonAnnotations.summary: Node {{NODE}} filesystem /mnt/stateful_partition almost out of space (0.6GB avail, SLO 2GB)
commonAnnotations.description: node_filesystem_avail_bytes on {{NODE}} (/mnt/stateful_partition) fell to 644MB, below the 1GB alert floor and the 2GB SLO. The node has begun reporting DiskPressure and is at risk of evicting {{WORKLOAD}} pods scheduled on it.
commonAnnotations.suspected_symptom: node filesystem on /mnt/stateful_partition exhausted; kubelet raising DiskPressure on {{NODE}}

adversarial_signals: container_memory_working_set_bytes for {{POD}} sits at 410MB against a 768MB limit — elevated but not OOM territory, a benign red herring that points away from memory.
A 'BackOff' event appears on {{POD2}} from a single image-pull retry 14m ago that already succeeded; it predates the incident and is unrelated.
{{NODE2}} shows MemoryPressure=False/DiskPressure=False with 11GB avail — a healthy peer node that could tempt a 'reschedule elsewhere' conclusion rather than fixing the disk on {{NODE}}.

pods.name: {{POD}}

conditions.message: node {{NODE}} has disk pressure; pod is a candidate for eviction

pods.note: writing upload buffers to local emptyDir / node /mnt/stateful_partition

events.message: Attempting to reclaim ephemeral-storage; node {{NODE}} nodefs.available 644Mi is below eviction threshold (imagefs/nodefs)
Failed to garbage collect required amount of images; nodefs still below threshold on /mnt/stateful_partition
The node was low on resource: ephemeral-storage. Container media-upload was using 3502Mi which exceeds its request of 1Gi.

lines: 2026-06-20T19:50:02Z INFO  media-upload buffering inbound upload chunk to /tmp/fill (local node fs)
2026-06-20T19:50:33Z WARN  disk usage on /mnt/stateful_partition at 92% (3.5Gi written to /tmp/fill, temp buffers not reaped)
2026-06-20T19:50:48Z ERROR DiskPressure: media-upload filesystem filling up
2026-06-20T19:50:49Z ERROR write /tmp/fill: no space left on device
2026-06-20T19:50:51Z ERROR DiskPressure: media-upload filesystem filling up

queries.note: 768Mi limit; elevated but well under OOM — adversarial

ruling_out_keywords: memory working set 410MB under 768Mi limit, not OOM
BackOff on {{POD2}} predates incident by 14m and already recovered
{{NODE2}} DiskPressure=False with 11.2GB avail — node hardware healthy, only {{NODE}} disk is full
node {{NODE}} Ready=True and MemoryPressure=False — not node_failure
answer.model_response:
ROOT_CAUSE: The media-upload workload wrote ~3.5GB of unbounded temp upload buffers to /tmp/fill on node {{NODE}}'s /mnt/stateful_partition, driving node_filesystem_avail_bytes down to 644MB (below the 2GB SLO and 1GB alert floor). The kubelet raised DiskPressure on {{NODE}}, began evicting media-upload pods ({{POD3}} evicted for ephemeral-storage), and writes now fail with 'no space left on device'. This is local disk/ephemeral-storage exhaustion on the node, not a memory, deploy, or node-hardware fault.
ROOT_CAUSE_CATEGORY: resource_exhaustion
VALIDATED_CLAIMS:
- Node {{NODE}} filesystem /mnt/stateful_partition is 96.6% full with only 644MB avail, below the 2GB SLO [evidence: k8s_node_health]
- node_filesystem_avail_bytes = 644000000 < 2000000000 SLO threshold, breached=true [evidence: prometheus_metrics]
- media-upload logged 'DiskPressure: media-upload filesystem filling up' and 'write /tmp/fill: no space left on device' after buffering 3.5Gi to /tmp/fill [evidence: k8s_pod_logs]
- Kubelet raised EvictionThresholdMet and evicted {{POD3}} for using 3502Mi ephemeral-storage vs its 1Gi request [evidence: k8s_events]
- NodeFilesystemAlmostOutOfSpace and DiskPressure alerts are firing for {{NODE}} [evidence: prometheus_alerts]
- Pod {{POD}} carries condition DiskPressure=True, candidate for eviction [evidence: k8s_pods]
NON_VALIDATED_CLAIMS:
- Memory exhaustion: container_memory_working_set_bytes is 410MB under a 768Mi limit — not OOM [evidence: prometheus_metrics]
- Image-pull failure: the BackOff on {{POD2}} is 14m old and already recovered, unrelated to current disk pressure [evidence: k8s_events]
- Node hardware failure: {{NODE}} is Ready=True with MemoryPressure=False; peer {{NODE2}} is fully healthy with 11.2GB free, so this is disk fill on one node, not a node failure [evidence: k8s_node_health]
CAUSAL_CHAIN:
- media-upload buffers unbounded upload data to /tmp/fill on the node-local /mnt/stateful_partition
- 3.5Gi of temp files are never reaped, filling the filesystem to 96.6%
- node_filesystem_avail_bytes drops to 644MB, crossing the 1GB alert floor and 2GB SLO
- kubelet on {{NODE}} reports DiskPressure and begins ephemeral-storage eviction
- {{POD3}} is evicted and {{POD}} writes fail with ENOSPC, degrading media-upload
- Fix: free the disk (remove /tmp/fill temp buffers) and restart the workload so avail_bytes recovers above the 2GB SLO

remediation.canonical_fix: kubectl exec -n rlvr-target deploy/media-upload -- rm -f /tmp/fill; kubectl rollout restart deployment/media-upload -n rlvr-target
